Neuromuscular Dentistry

When your bite is off,
your entire jaw feels it.

Neuromuscular dentistry focuses on the harmony between your teeth, jaw joints, muscles and airway to restore comfort, function and long-term stability.

Start Assessment
Let's Assess

Are you experiencing
these symptoms?

Tap the ones that feel familiar. If even two or three apply, a neuromuscular evaluation is worth a conversation.

Selected Symptoms: 0

Root-Cause Analysis

Understanding
the jaw-muscle
connection.Understanding the
jaw-muscle connection.

Most jaw pain isn't random. It follows a pattern, and once you can see it, the path to relief becomes obvious.

01

Bite Imbalance

Misaligned teeth or jaw positions create uneven loading across the jaw and muscular system.

02

Muscle Compensation

Surrounding muscles work harder to compensate, creating chronic tension, fatigue and dysfunction.

03

Pain & Dysfunction

Sustained strain leads to joint inflammation, nerve sensitivity and symptoms that can radiate into the neck, head and shoulders.

Scope of Care

Common conditions we address.

01

TMJ / TMD Disorders

Dysfunction of the temporomandibular joint, including disc displacement, inflammation and joint degeneration.

02

Bruxism

Chronic teeth grinding and clenching that causes tooth wear, jaw fatigue and muscle overload.

03

Facial Muscle Pain

Myofascial pain affecting the face, temples and jaw muscles.

04

Bite Imbalance

Malocclusion patterns where teeth do not function together harmoniously.

05

Chronic Jaw Tension

Persistent muscle tightness and guarding in the masseter, temporalis and pterygoid muscles.

06

Occlusal Dysfunction

Uneven dental forces that affect chewing efficiency, tooth wear and long-term structural health.

07

Airway-Related Jaw Issues

Jaw positions that influence breathing patterns and contribute to airway narrowing or sleep-related breathing concerns.

08

Sleep-Related Concerns

Neuromuscular conditions linked to airway compromise and disrupted sleep architecture.

Scroll for more

About Dr. Amruta Godbole

Tooth be told.

Dr. Amruta Godbole's training in neuromuscular and functional dentistry sits at the intersection of restoration, occlusion and airway. Her approach is diagnostic before it is corrective, the bite, the joints, the muscles and the way you breathe at night are all part of the same picture.

Patients walk in with headaches, ear pressure, clicking jaws or grinding habits, and walk out with a clear explanation of what is causing them and a layered plan to fix it, starting with the least invasive option that actually works.

Treatment Pathways

Possible treatments.

We sequence treatments from the least invasive upward. Most patients find relief long before they reach the bottom of the list.

  • 01Custom Orthotics & Splints
  • 02Bite Stabilization
  • 03Occlusal Adjustment
  • 04Muscle Relaxation Therapy
  • 05Full-Mouth Rehabilitation
  • 06Airway-Focused Planning
  • 07Restorative Correction
  • 08Night Guards
  • 09Anti-Snoring Devices

Scroll for more

We'd love to meet you.
